data.title: AI | Musk Ally Antonio Gracias Seeks AI, Energy Bets After SpaceX Windfall
data.description: Antonio Gracias, a longtime SpaceX board member, is looking for more investments in artificial intelligence, energy and space after bets in Elon Musk’s company netted his fund nearly $100 billion, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Circle CEO Says Open USD Backers Matter Only If Token Delivers Regulated Transaction Flow
data.description:
Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire said Open USD’s more than 140 backers would matter only if the token can turn distribution into live, regulated transaction flow.

According to NS3.AI, Open Standard said OUSD would use partner governance and share reserve earnings after a management fee.

Allaire also pointed to USDC’s integrations, liquidity, licensing footprint, CCTP, and Gateway as elements of its competitive moat.

data.title: Strategy Launches Digital Credit Capital Framework as STRC Targets 12% Dividends
data.description:
Strategy has launched a Digital Credit Capital Framework that includes plans to raise STRC dividends to 12% and authorize Bitcoin sales. According to NS3.AI, Farside Investors said STRC carries structural risks linked to adjustable coupon rates and price stabilization mechanisms.

STRC recently traded around $75 before rebounding to $86.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Index Rises 0.2% to 101.39 on July 2
data.description:
The U.S. Dollar Index, which tracks the dollar against six major currencies, rose 0.2% on July 2 to close at 101.39 in late trading.

According to ChainCatcher, the euro fell to $1.138 from $1.142 in the previous session, while the British pound rose to $1.3278 from $1.3255.

The dollar slipped to 162.53 Japanese yen from 162.62, and strengthened to 0.8092 Swiss francs from 0.8083.

It also rose to 1.4216 Canadian dollars from 1.4204 and to 9.73 Swedish kronor from 9.7005.

data.title: Hyperscale Data Buys 49.249 Bitcoin for About $2.9 Million
data.description:
Hyperscale Data has purchased 49.249 BTC, valued at approximately $2.9 million.

According to NS3.AI, the company is a Bitcoin mining firm listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: Farside Investors Flags Structural Risks in Strategy’s STRC Preferred Product
data.description:
Farside Investors said Strategy’s STRC preferred product carries structural risks tied to its adjustable dividend rate and price-stabilization mechanism. According to Odaily, STRC was issued at about $100 and is designed to raise dividends when the price falls below $100 and lower dividends when the price rises above $100, aiming to guide the market price back toward that level.

Farside Investors said that if investor concerns about Strategy’s credit risk increase, STRC’s price could decline. It added that raising dividends to support the price could increase cash pressure and further affect investor certainty in valuing the security.

STRC recently traded at about $75 before rebounding to $86.

Strategy has introduced a Digital Credit Capital Framework that includes plans to build U.S. dollar reserves, raise STRC dividends to 12%, repurchase preferred securities at a discount, and authorize the sale of Bitcoin to help fund dividends and reserves.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Meituan Unveils LongCat-2.0 Open-License AI Model With Lower API Pricing Than GPT-5.5
data.description:
Meituan has unveiled LongCat-2.0, an open-license AI model with 1.6 trillion parameters.

According to NS3.AI, the model had been running on OpenRouter under the name Owl Alpha.

The report said standard API pricing is set at $0.75 per million input tokens and $2.95 per million output tokens. It also compared those rates with GPT-5.5 at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens, and Claude Sonnet 5’s introductory pricing of $2 per million input tokens and $10 per million output tokens.

data.title: Golden Seeds Tops $200 Million Invested in Women-Led Companies, CEO Says
data.description:
Golden Seeds has surpassed $200 million invested in women-led companies, according to Bloomberg, citing comments from Chief Executive Officer Jo Ann Corkran.

Corkran said the investment landscape has changed for women entrepreneurs seeking initial backing, and that investors are looking for tech companies that can be easily leveraged as well as green technology.

data.title: Apple in Talks to Buy Memory Chips From Chinese Suppliers on Pentagon Blacklist
data.description:
Apple Inc. is negotiating to buy memory chips from two Chinese semiconductor makers that are on a Pentagon blacklist as it seeks to reduce the impact of a global memory shortage, according to Bloomberg.

The shortage has forced Apple to raise prices across its product line.

data.title: Wall Street Looks Beyond Bear Market as Clarity Talks Continue and Trump’s Crypto Income Draws Criticism
data.description:
A Fox Business crypto reporter said Wall Street is shifting its focus beyond the bear market, while enforcement groups continue holding discussions on Clarity and U.S. President Donald Trump’s crypto income has drawn criticism from Senate Democrats.

According to Odaily, the reporter posted the update on X, describing ongoing talks involving enforcement groups and noting the Democratic criticism in the Senate.

data.title: Apple Reportedly Negotiates With Trump Administration on Chip Procurement
data.description: Apple is in talks with the Trump administration regarding chip procurement, according to Jin10.

data.title: Empery Digital Considers Selling Part of 3,000 Bitcoin Holdings to Fund $65 Million AI Data Center Project
data.description:
Empery Digital (EMPD) may sell part of its 3,000 Bitcoin holdings to finance a $65 million AI data center project.

According to Odaily, BitcoinTreasuries.NET said in a post on X that Empery Digital is considering the sale as part of its funding plan.

The post added that the company currently has no plans to continue increasing its Bitcoin holdings.

data.title: STOCKS | Nasdaq Golden Dragon China Index Rises 2.9% as Micron and SanDisk Drop Over 10%
data.description:
U.S. stocks closed mixed on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Golden Dragon China Index rising nearly 3% while several chipmakers fell sharply.

According to Jin10, preliminary figures showed the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.02%, the S&P 500 fell 0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite declined 0.66%.

Among major stocks, Nvidia fell 1%. Micron Technology dropped 10.5% and SanDisk fell 10.6%. AMD declined 6.8%, while Meta rose 8.8%.

The Nasdaq Golden Dragon China Index climbed 2.9%. Alibaba gained 2%.

data.title: STOCKS | Tesla Draws Musk Meme Traders as Shares Hit $420
data.description: Tesla Inc. shares hit $420, drawing renewed interest from traders tied to Elon Musk-era meme-stock culture, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Dan Ives to Leave Wedbush to Launch AI-Focused Merchant Bank Venture
data.description:
Technology analyst Dan Ives said he will leave Wedbush Securities to start a new venture aimed at building a “modern merchant bank” focused on artificial intelligence opportunities.

According to Odaily, Ives said the new institution will combine research, investment banking advisory, capital raising, and investment activities.

He said the organization will provide integrated financial and research services for companies and investors, centered on structural changes driven by AI.

data.title: Barron’s 400 ETF Sees $600 Million Inflow, Balchunas Says
data.description:
Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas said in a post on X that BFOR, the Barron’s 400 ETF, recorded a large inflow of funds.

According to Odaily, Balchunas said the 13-year-old fund, which had about $200 million in assets, then suddenly received $600 million in inflows.

He added that the ETF has outperformed the broader market so far this year and said it may have attracted a model, though he was not certain.

Balchunas said this indicates an ETF’s size can expand to four times its original level without affecting its premium rate.

data.title: Iran’s Parliament Speaker Denies Inspectors Entered Bombed Sites
data.description: Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said claims that inspectors entered bombed locations were false. According to Jin10, Ghalibaf described the statements about inspectors accessing the bombed sites as incorrect.

data.title: STOCKS | Michael Burry’s Short Call Sends Caterpillar Down From Record High
data.description: Caterpillar Inc. shares slid from a record high Wednesday after investor Michael Burry said he shorted the industrial company for the first time, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Moody’s Warns Trump’s June 22 Orders Delay Post-Quantum Deadlines to 2030–31, Raising Pressure on Crypto Firms
data.description:
Moody’s Ratings said U.S. President Donald Trump’s June 22 executive orders shift federal post-quantum preparedness deadlines to 2030-31, increasing pressure on digital asset firms.

According to NS3.AI, Moody’s warned that Bitcoin’s public-key cryptography could face serious risk from a sufficiently capable quantum computer.

Moody’s said the delayed timelines heighten the need for digital asset companies to address potential quantum-related security threats.

data.title: Gulf States Reportedly Pursue Separate Regional Security Talks With Iran
data.description:
Saudi Arabia, Oman, and Qatar have been seeking direct diplomatic contacts and negotiations with Iran during U.S.-Iran talks, according to a report by the UK’s The Daily Telegraph dated June 30.

According to Jin10, the report said Gulf states were adjusting their relationship with Iran as U.S. credibility declined.

After Iran and the United States signed a memorandum of understanding, Iran and Oman held their first meeting to discuss management of the Strait of Hormuz, the report said.

It added that further talks were expected among Oman and Qatar, Iran and Saudi Arabia, and Qatar and Saudi Arabia, with the aim of determining how the region would coexist after the end of hostilities.

The consultations were reported to cover plans for managing navigation through the Strait of Hormuz and potential economic incentives Gulf states could offer Iran in exchange for corresponding security concessions.

The report said these discussions were taking place alongside, but independently from, the U.S.-Iran negotiations.
